explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A case study details Ovintiv's use of machine learning for operational decision-making and optimization in end-of-life wells. The report highlights the project's focus on actionable results and the integration of machine learning into production processes, indicating a structured approach to AI implementation.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This case study provides evidence for the **governance** pillar by detailing Ovintiv's use of machine learning for operational decision-making and optimization in end-of-life wells. The report highlights the project's focus on actionable results and the integration of ML into production processes, indicating a structured approach to AI implementation. Additionally, the case study touches upon **transparency** by mentioning the importance of communicating model reliability to operational teams, suggesting an effort to ensure understanding and trust in the AI system.
relevant_sources.title: Case Study: Optimizing Production in End-of-Life Wells with Machine Learning

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: A case study references the importance of communicating model reliability to operational teams. This suggests an effort to ensure understanding and trust in the AI system.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This case study provides evidence for the **governance** pillar by detailing Ovintiv's use of machine learning for operational decision-making and optimization in end-of-life wells. The report highlights the project's focus on actionable results and the integration of ML into production processes, indicating a structured approach to AI implementation. Additionally, the case study touches upon **transparency** by mentioning the importance of communicating model reliability to operational teams, suggesting an effort to ensure understanding and trust in the AI system.
relevant_sources.title: Case Study: Optimizing Production in End-of-Life Wells with Machine Learning

summary.overall_findings: Ovintiv's published materials detail the use of machine learning for operational decision-making and optimization in end-of-life wells, an example of its approach to governance. This is one of two responsible AI pillars addressed in its public disclosures, out of seven evaluated, with transparency also noted through a case study on communicating model reliability to operational teams. Drawing from 8 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
